title: pinguicula vulgaris subsp. corsica
intro: Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. corsica, commonly known as the Corsican butterwort, is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the family Lentibulariaceae. It is native to the island of Corsica and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Pinguicula vulgaris subsp. corsica include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory ailments. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
